# Decisions: billable-service-scope

## Decision log

### D001 - Source of truth is project-level specs/SERVICES.md, stamped into each SPEC

**Date:** 2026-07-14

**Status:** Accepted

**Context:** The feature request allowed either `specs/SERVICES.md` or per-SPEC frontmatter as the declaration point for contracted services.

**Decision:** `specs/SERVICES.md` is the single project-level source of truth, generated by `/project-init`. `/spec-create` stamps a read-only "Contracted services" line into each new SPEC for local visibility, but the file always wins on conflict.

**Reasoning:** A client contract spans the whole project, not one feature. Per-spec declaration would be re-asked on every feature and drift silently. The existing SPEC template has no YAML frontmatter, so a markdown section keeps template consistency.

**Consequences:** Skills that need the declaration read one well-known path. Specs created before SERVICES.md existed carry the conservative-default note until the file is created.

### D002 - Canonical service names mirror the agency service catalog (13 names)

**Date:** 2026-07-14

**Status:** Accepted

**Context:** Service names must match what sales actually sells, or the proposal, invoice, and technical scope will disagree.

**Decision:** The vocabulary is exactly the 13 services from the agency's Services and Packages catalog: web, seo, geo, aeo, ai-visibility, cro, analytics, crm-integration, ai-chatbot, ai-follow-up-automation, ai-commercial-proposals, technical-support, growth-strategy. Unrecognized names are treated as not contracted and flagged.

**Reasoning:** One shared vocabulary end-to-end (sales → contract → SDD) is the whole point of the feature. ("website-development" from the catalog maps to `web` — the framework's historical term.)

**Consequences:** Catalog changes require updating `specs/_templates/SERVICES.md`; the list lives in exactly one file to make that cheap.

### D003 - seo-review moves to a new overlay profile, not a disabled flag or a doc-only warning

**Date:** 2026-07-14

**Status:** Accepted

**Context:** Three ways to stop bundling SEO with web installs: remove the skill from the profile entirely, mark it disabled, or keep it installed but warn in docs.

**Decision:** Create `seo-geo-addon` as a standard optional overlay profile (default: false, NOT disabled), following the `messaging-event-driven` precedent exactly, and remove `seo-review` from `next-prisma-web`.

**Reasoning:** Installing a paid-service capability by default invites free use regardless of warnings. `disabled: true` (blockchain-crypto pattern) would block legitimate installs when the service IS sold. The overlay mechanism already exists, requires zero installer code changes, and maps 1:1 to the commercial model: sold → install it, not sold → it isn't there.

**Consequences:** Existing installed projects keep their current skills until re-installed (accepted). README profiles table needs updating. GEO/AI-visibility reviewers are declared as plannedSkills for roadmap visibility.

### D004 - Missing SERVICES.md defaults to "no billable add-ons contracted"

**Date:** 2026-07-14

**Status:** Accepted

**Context:** Pre-existing projects and projects where `/project-init` was never run have no SERVICES.md. Skills need a defined behavior.

**Decision:** Absence is treated as web-only: no billable add-on is considered contracted; skills state this explicitly and suggest running `/project-init`.

**Reasoning:** The failure mode of the permissive default (assume contracted) is giving away revenue silently; the failure mode of the conservative default is a one-line prompt to declare services. The conservative default is strictly cheaper.

**Consequences:** Teams on old projects must create SERVICES.md once (or run `/project-init`) before SEO-family recommendations resume.

### D005 - Baseline-quality vs. billable boundary lives in the CONSTITUTION template

**Date:** 2026-07-14

**Status:** Accepted

**Context:** Some technical-SEO overlaps are genuinely base web quality (unique title/description, semantic HTML, alt text, accessibility, reasonable performance) — a site without them is defective, not "un-SEO'd". The line must be drawn somewhere explicit or every review becomes a negotiation.

**Decision:** The boundary table ships in `specs/_templates/CONSTITUTION.md` (Billing boundary section): included quality = unique title/description per page, semantic HTML, alt text, responsive, accessibility, reasonable performance; billable = JSON-LD/schema.org, sitemap/robots strategy, OG/Twitter optimization, hreflang, strategic canonicals, keyword research, llms.txt, GEO entity work, ranking-oriented CWV optimization. Owners may edit it per project during `/project-init`.

**Reasoning:** The constitution is read by every downstream skill, making it the one durable backstop even if a future skill forgets the conditional. Making it owner-editable acknowledges the boundary is a business judgment, not a technical constant.

**Consequences:** The same table should be mirrored in the agency's commercial "Services and Packages" document so sales and delivery state the same boundary.

### D006 - Harden seo-review in place now; build a real `seo` agent later as its own feature

**Date:** 2026-07-15

**Status:** Accepted

**Context:** A coverage audit of `skills/seo-review/SKILL.md`, requested directly by the user before letting this feature proceed, found two defects: it opens by delegating to an `agents/seo.md` that does not exist (only `deep-reasoner.md` and `fast-worker.md` exist), so the checklist fallback is what always actually runs; and the checklist itself only covers technical on-page SEO — hreflang is named in the description but has zero checklist items, and AEO/GEO/AI-visibility are not covered at all. Shipping `seo-geo-addon` as-is would sell three services (SEO, GEO, AEO) via one skill that delivers roughly half of one.

**Decision:** Two changes, both scoped into this feature rather than spun off: (1) remove the dead "Delegation to SEO agent" block from `seo-review/SKILL.md` instead of building the missing agent now — a real `seo` agent is deferred to its own future feature; (2) add the missing hreflang / internationalization checklist section to close the promised-but-unchecked gap. AEO/GEO/AI-visibility coverage is explicitly NOT built now — `seo-geo-addon`'s `plannedSkills` is expanded from two entries to three (`aeo-review`, `geo-review`, `ai-visibility-review`) so the profile's roadmap honestly reflects that these are three separate future reviewers, not sub-features of `seo-review`.

**Reasoning:** Removing dead text is zero behavioral change (the fallback checklist is what always executed) and unblocks this feature without taking on the scope of designing a real agent under time pressure. Fixing hreflang is a small, well-defined checklist addition matching what the skill already claims to do. Building AEO/GEO/AI-visibility reviewers now would be a much larger, separately-scoped effort (each has its own evaluation criteria distinct from technical SEO) and belongs in its own future spec, not bundled into a billing-scope feature — this feature's job is to stop the addon from *overselling*, not to build the missing reviewers.

**Consequences:** `seo-geo-addon`, once implemented, ships an honestly-scoped `seo-review` (technical SEO + hreflang) plus three declared `plannedSkills` placeholders; anyone reading `profiles.json` sees the true state instead of an implied "SEO/GEO/AEO covered" claim. Building `aeo-review`, `geo-review`, `ai-visibility-review`, and a real `agents/seo.md` remain open future work, each warranting its own SPEC when prioritized.
